medications.indication_rationale: Interim bronchodilation via spacer (e.g. 4 puffs, repeated as needed per an asthma action plan) while arranging urgent in-person assessment/transport.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 4 puffs via spacer, repeat every 20 minutes as needed while awaiting assessment (adult, per asthma first-aid)

medications.indication_rationale: A short oral corticosteroid course is standard for exacerbations, but for a reliever-refractory nocturnal attack in a patient alone it should accompany urgent in-person assessment, not replace it.
No fever/purulent sputum; infection is not evident.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Sit upright, use the reliever through a spacer as directed, have your sister come over or stay on the phone, and prepare to call 000 if breathing worsens.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Optimises drug delivery and ensures rapid escalation for a patient alone.

patient_education_points.point: Because your reliever isn't working and you're breathless lying down, you need to be assessed in person now — I can't listen to your chest or give nebulised treatment over video.
While arranging that, use your reliever through a spacer (4 puffs, repeat every 20 minutes) sitting upright, and get your sister to come over.
Call 000 immediately if you can only speak a few words at a time, your lips go blue, you become drowsy or exhausted, or the reliever stops working entirely.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Exacerbation severity and response, maintenance inhaled corticosteroid escalation, and EGPA work-up (eosinophils, ANCA, cardiac/renal/neuro screen).
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Unable to speak in sentences, cyanosis, drowsiness, or reliever failure — call 000.

must_include_items: Recognise reliever-refractory asthma as needing urgent in-person/ED assessment
Give interim spacer reliever dosing and clear 000 criteria
Do not endorse home-only management for a patient alone with a refractory nocturnal attack
Flag EGPA prodrome for later work-up
acceptable_alternatives: Direct 000 if the patient deteriorates or cannot arrange transport
Short oral corticosteroid course alongside in-person assessment
errors_of_commission: Reassuring home-only management of a reliever-refractory nocturnal attack
Attributing dyspnoea to anxiety and under-treating
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: High-risk case: passing REQUIRES urgent in-person/ED assessment plus interim reliever and 000 criteria. Endorsing home-only management is a failure.
